html:
Feathers are Items obtained by killing Birds. There are four feather types; Jet Feather, Crimson Feather, Azure Feather, and Saffron Feather. They can be obtained by killing birds by weapons or by "Murdering" them in the inventory after capturing them alive. Each bird has a 50% chance to drop its feather, excluding Pengulls, which have only a 20% chance of dropping theirs.

Contents

  • 1 Jet Feather
  • 2 Crimson Feather
  • 3 Azure Feather
  • 4 Saffron Feather
  • 5 Usage
  • 6 Trivia
  • 7 Gallery

Jet Feather

Exclusive to:  

Crimson Feather

Exclusive to:  

Azure Feather

Exclusive to:  

Saffron Feather

Exclusive to:

A New Reign added a new type of feather to Don't Starve Together, the Saffron Feather, which is dropped by killing Canaries (9% chance) and used to craft Electric Darts. Another way to obtain Saffron Feathers is to bring Canaries to the Caves. The Canaries will be poisoned after spending some time in a Birdcage in the Caves. After they are poisoned, they will drop 5-6 Saffron Feathers if they are set free in the overworld, or 1-2 Saffron Feathers if they are made to explode in the Caves. Feathers can be used as fuel, providing about 15 seconds of fire in a Fire Pit.

All 3 feather types can be found by destroying Tumbleweeds or as a "gift" from a Catcoon. Tumbleweeds have a mere 0.33% chance of dropping up to 3 Feathers. The DLC also introduces Buzzards, which have a 33% chance of dropping a Jet Feather when killed.

Usage

×20

Trivia

  • Before the Strange New Powers update, Crimson Feathers were called Redbird Feathers, Jet Feathers were called Crow Feathers, and Azure Feathers were called Snowbird Feathers. This has been changed thanks to the addition of Pengulls since they also drop Jet Feathers.
  • Saffron Feathers were introduced in the Warts and All update.

Gallery

  • Jet Feather Without Shadow
  • Crimson Feather Without Shadow
  • Azure Feather Without Shadow
  • Saffron Feather Without Shadow
  • Original HD Jet Feather icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Original HD Crimson Feather icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
plainText:
Don't Starve
Together
Feathers are Items obtained by killing Birds. There are four feather types; Jet Feather, Crimson Feather, Azure Feather, and Saffron Feather. They can be obtained by killing birds by weapons or by "Murdering" them in the inventory after capturing them alive. Each bird has a 50% chance to drop its feather, excluding Pengulls, which have only a 20% chance of dropping theirs.
Contents
1 Jet Feather
2 Crimson Feather
3 Azure Feather
4 Saffron Feather
5 Usage
6 Trivia
7 Gallery
Jet Feather[edit | edit source]
Jet Feather
Object Info
7.5 sec
Can be Burned

Exclusive to:
“A crow feather.”
–Wilson, when examining a Jet Feather.
“Crow feather. It probably smells terrible on fire.”
–Willow, when examining a Jet Feather.
“Is feather of bird black.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Jet Feather.
“A crow feather, black like eternal darkness.”
–Wendy, when examining a Jet Feather.
“IT HAS BEEN SEPARATED FROM ITS BIRD”
–WX-78, when examining a Jet Feather.
“Feather of Corvus.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Jet Feather.
“A gross feather.”
–Woodie, when examining a Jet Feather.
“Black is the color of sleep.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Jet Feather.
“Interesting! Feathers appear to have properties wholly unique from each other.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Jet Feather.
“A töken fröm the ravens!”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Jet Feather.
“Feather of black.”
–Webber, when examining a Jet Feather.
“A real softy.”
–Walani, when examining a Jet Feather.
“A bird's feather, in truffle black.”
–Warly, when examining a Jet Feather.
“Thet feather be mighty oily.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Jet Feather.
“'TIS BIRDY PART”
–Wilba, when examining a Jet Feather.
“Caw Tweeter clothes”
–Wormwood, when examining a Jet Feather.
“I'll keep it to remind me of how I'm not flying.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Jet Feather.
“Not a whole lotta use for that.”
–Winona, when examining a Jet Feather.
“A memento of my jet feathered friend.”
–Wortox, when examining a Jet Feather.
“Black birdy feather.”
–Wurt, when examining a Jet Feather.
“Looks like a black bird feather.”
–Walter, when examining a Jet Feather.
“A black feather.”
–Wanda, when examining a Jet Feather.

Exclusive to:
“A redbird feather.”
–Wilson, when examining a Crimson Feather.
“Redbird feather. It probably smells terrible on fire.”
–Willow, when examining a Crimson Feather.
“Is feather of bird red.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a Crimson Feather.
“A redbird feather, red like blood.”
–Wendy, when examining a Crimson Feather.
“IT HAS BEEN SEPARATED FROM ITS BIRD”
–WX-78, when examining a Crimson Feather.
“Feather of Cardinalis.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a Crimson Feather.
“A disgusting feather.”
–Woodie, when examining a Crimson Feather.
“Red is the color of fire.”
–Maxwell, when examining a Crimson Feather.
“Epidermal growth with several exploitable properties.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a Crimson Feather.
“Red as my hair.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a Crimson Feather.
“Feather of red.”
–Webber, when examining a Crimson Feather.
“I'm tickled by the color of this.”
–Walani, when examining a Crimson Feather.
“A bird's feather, in cherry red.”
–Warly, when examining a Crimson Feather.
“Me feet are tickle proof.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a Crimson Feather.
“'TIS BIRDY PART”
–Wilba, when examining a Crimson Feather.
“Red Tweeter clothes”
–Wormwood, when examining a Crimson Feather.
“Maybe if I cover myself in feathers I can fly away from here.”
–Wheeler, when examining a Crimson Feather.
“Kinda useless. Looks nice, anyway.”
–Winona, when examining a Crimson Feather.
“To remind myself of its sweet beaky face.”
–Wortox, when examining a Crimson Feather.
“Red birdy feather.”
–Wurt, when examining a Crimson Feather.
“Looks like a redbird feather.”
–Walter, when examining a Crimson Feather.
“A red feather.”
–Wanda, when examining a Crimson Feather.

“Is feather of bird white.”
–Wolfgang, when examining an Azure Feather.
“A snowbird feather, white like the light at the end of the tunnel.”
–Wendy, when examining an Azure Feather.
“IT HAS BEEN SEPARATED FROM ITS BIRD”
–WX-78, when examining an Azure Feather.
“It looks like a feather of Cardinalis, but white.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining an Azure Feather.
“A stupid feather.”
–Woodie, when examining an Azure Feather.
“Grey is the color of pain.”
–Maxwell, when examining an Azure Feather.
“Only available in the cold season.”
–Wagstaff, when examining an Azure Feather.
“Winter's feather.”
–Wigfrid, when examining an Azure Feather.
“Feather of white.”
–Webber, when examining an Azure Feather.
“I would love a necklace made of these.”
–Walani, when examining an Azure Feather.
“A bird's feather, in tuna blue.”
–Warly, when examining an Azure Feather.
“Whar did all th'colors go?”
–Woodlegs, when examining an Azure Feather.
“'TIS BIRDY PART”
–Wilba, when examining an Azure Feather.
“Snow Tweeter clothes”
–Wormwood, when examining an Azure Feather.
“A feather that doesn't let me fly? That hardly seems fair.”
–Wheeler, when examining an Azure Feather.
“If only I had a cap to put it in.”
–Winona, when examining an Azure Feather.
“A keepsake of a gentle blue friend.”
–Wortox, when examining an Azure Feather.
“Snowy bird feather.”
–Wurt, when examining an Azure Feather.
“Looks like a snowbird feather.”
–Walter, when examining an Azure Feather.
“A white-ish feather.”
–Wanda, when examining an Azure Feather.

A New Reign added a new type of feather to Don't Starve Together, the Saffron Feather, which is dropped by killing Canaries (9% chance) and used to craft Electric Darts. Another way to obtain Saffron Feathers is to bring Canaries to the Caves. The Canaries will be poisoned after spending some time in a Birdcage in the Caves. After they are poisoned, they will drop 5-6 Saffron Feathers if they are set free in the overworld, or 1-2 Saffron Feathers if they are made to explode in the Caves.
Feathers can be used as fuel, providing about 15 seconds of fire in a Fire Pit.
All 3 feather types can be found by destroying Tumbleweeds or as a "gift" from a Catcoon. Tumbleweeds have a mere 0.33% chance of dropping up to 3 Feathers. The DLC also introduces Buzzards, which have a 33% chance of dropping a Jet Feather when killed.

It probably smells terrible on fire. |wolfgang = Is feather of bird yellow. |wendy = A canary feather, golden as the gilded cage. |wx78 = FOUL ORGANICS LEAVE THEIR PIECES EVERYWHERE |wickerbottom = Feather of Canaria. |woodie = A grody feather. |maxwell = Yellow is the color of naivety. |wigfrid = In remembrance of a söngbird. |webber = Feather of yellow. |winona = That's not a good sign. |warly = A bird's feather, in lemon yellow. |wortox = A token, to remember its sweet song by. |wormwood = Tweeter clothes" |wurt = Yellow birdy feather. |walter = Looks like a canary feather. |wanda = A yellow feather. |choose = wx78 |source = when examining a Saffron Feather.}} ''[[A New Reign]]'' added a new type of feather to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', the '''Saffron Feather''', which is dropped by killing [[Birds|Canaries]] (9% chance) and used to craft [[Electric Dart]]s. Another way to obtain Saffron Feathers is to bring Canaries to the [[Caves]]. The Canaries will be poisoned after spending some time in a [[Birdcage]] in the Caves. After they are poisoned, they will drop 5-6 Saffron Feathers if they are set free in the overworld, or 1-2 Saffron Feathers if they are made to explode in the Caves. Feathers can be used as fuel, providing about 15 seconds of fire in a [[Fire Pit]]. All 3 feather types can be found by destroying [[Tumbleweed]]s or as a "gift" from a [[Catcoon]]. [[Tumbleweed]]s have a mere 0.33% chance of dropping up to 3 Feathers. The DLC also introduces [[Buzzards]], which have a 33% chance of dropping a Jet Feather when killed. {{Clear}} =={{pic32|Icon Tools}} Usage == {{Recipe |item1 = Jet Feather |item2 = Charcoal |item3 = Twigs |tool = Science Machine |result = Feather Pencil}} {{Recipe |item1 = Jet Feather |item2 = Bone Shards |count2 = 2 |item3 = Twigs |count3 = 2 |tool = Alchemy Engine |result = Saddlehorn}} {{Recipe |item1 = Jet Feather |item2 = Stinger |item3 = Cut Reeds |count3 = 2 |tool = Science Machine |result = Sleep Dart}} {{Recipe |item1 = Crimson Feather |item2 = Charcoal |item3 = Cut Reeds |count3 = 2 |tool = Science Machine |result = Fire Dart}} {{Recipe |item1 = Azure Feather |item2 = Hound's Tooth |item3 = Cut Reeds |count3 = 2 |tool = Science Machine |result = Blow Dart}} {{Recipe |item1 = Jet Feather |count1 = 3 |item2 = Crimson Feather |count2 = 2 |item3 = Tentacle Spots |count3 = 2 |tool = Alchemy Engine |result = Feather Hat}} {{Recipe |item1 = Crimson Feather |count1 = 3 |item2 = Rope |item3 = Pig Skin |count3 = 2 |tool = Science Machine |result = Summer Frest}} {{Recipe |item1 = Saffron Feather |item2 = Cut Reeds |count2 = 2 |item3 = Gold Nugget |tool = Science Machine |result = Electric Dart}} {{Recipe |item1 = Jet Feather |tool = Tackle Receptacle |result = Jet Quill Float}} {{Recipe |item1 = Crimson Feather |tool = Tackle Receptacle |result = Crimson Quill Float}} {{Recipe |item1 = Azure Feather |tool = Tackle Receptacle |result = Azure Quill Float}} {{Recipe |item1 = Saffron Feather |tool = Tackle Receptacle |result = Saffron Quill Float}} {{Recipe |item3 = Black Flag |count3 = 1 |item1 = Saffron Feather |count1 = 1 |item2 = Captain's Tricorn |count2 = 1 |result = Polly Roger's Hat |tool = Blueprint (rare)}} {{Recipe |item1 = Saffron Feather |item2 = Scrap |result = Shockscrap Shots |resultcount = 20 |character = Walter |skill = Utility Rounds}} =={{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * Before the [[New Features#July 2.2C 2013 -
